industryHighlights: Semiconductor demand is increasingly driven by AI data centers, stressing supply chains.
Vision inspection and digital twins are moving from pilots to standard work in factories.
Execs are prioritizing AI literacy and internal training over pure tool rollouts.
bestPerformers: NVDA – GPU demand from AI datacenters continues to outpace supply.
TSMC – Benefiting from advanced node demand tied to AI workloads.
ASML – EUV backlog supported by HBM and leading-edge logic ramps.
worstPerformers: Legacy CPU-only vendors – Losing share to AI-optimized architectures.
Single-sourced fabs – Struggling with supply assurance vs. dual-sourced peers.
Pure-play memory suppliers – Margin pressure from cyclical oversupply.
upcomingIPOs: EdgeAI Systems – Edge inference hardware for industrial automation (expected 2H 2026).
DataFabric Cloud – Data pipeline SaaS optimized for AI training workloads.
FactoryVision AI – Vertical computer-vision platform for discrete manufacturing.
aiToolsReminder: Have you tried the AI tools on Vidhai yet? Start with MeetPilot for automatic meeting notes, action items, and follow-ups — built on the same stack that powers this newsletter.
